title: Opeli Mors
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=New Republic |image= |name=Opeli Mors |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory II: Rebirth]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=She/her |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin=[[Color/Legends|Dark]] |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Jin'ri trade syndicate]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I know your type. Profit is one thing. You people ''gouge'' until there's nothing left, then abandon your charges when they can no longer pay.''"
"''Not true. We underwrite charity cases with monies we make from those who can afford our services. If we could operate on an entirely altruistic level, we would.|Leia Organa Solo and Opeli Mors|}} '''Opeli Mors''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] member of the newly formed [[Jin'ri trade syndicate]] during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]. In [[29 ABY]], Mors served as the syndicate's representative in a discussion involving the formation of a network to aid [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] movement across war-torn regions of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. The meeting, held at [[Shelter]] station, was attended by [[Bana Besadii Diori]], [[Numa Rar]], [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], [[Han Solo/Legends|Han]], [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]] and [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] at [[Shelter]] station. She offered her group's services in supplying troops and [[Starship/Legends|ships]] for a price. ==Biography== {{Quote|We are a relatively new concern. We formed to meet the needs of refugees soon after the fall of Duro. We welcome the opportunity to expand.|Opeli Mors|}} Opeli Mors was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] and a member of the [[Jin'ri trade syndicate]], a newly formed group of war profiteers established after the fall of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Duro/Legends|Duro]] during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]—a conflict fought between the extragalactic [[Yuuzhan Vong]] and the [[New Republic]]—to meet the needs of the displaced refugees. In [[29 ABY]], Mors was sent by the syndicate as its representative in a meeting that involved the creation of a network to aid refugees across war-torn regions of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. The meeting was held at [[Shelter]], a [[Space station/Legends|space station]] located in [[Maw Cluster/Legends|the Maw]] and owned by the businessman [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]]. Other attendees were [[Bana Besadii Diori]] of the [[Hutt underground]], [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Numa Rar]] of the [[New Plympto/Legends|New Plympto]] [[New Plympto resistance (Yuuzhan Vong War)|resistance]], and members of the [[Solo family/Legends|Solo family]]: [[Han Solo/Legends|Han]], [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]] and [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]]. Mors supported the creation of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]] [[Great River]] because of the profits the syndicate would make selling supplies along the pathway.{{CSWECite|II|332|Mors, Opeli}} ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|I came here in good faith.|Opeli Mors|}} Mors had a business-like personality and was calm during heated negotiations. When Leia was accusing her organization of taking advantage of war refugees by gouging them with high prices, Mors did not rise to the provocation and kept her replies calm and steady. Though driven by profit-minded pragmatism, Mors demonstrated flexibility when diplomacy required it, balancing her syndicate’s interests with a willingness to assist broader humanitarian efforts by offering a starship free of charge. ==Behind the scenes== Opeli Mors first appeared in [[2001]] in ''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory II: Rebirth]]'', the second novel in [[Greg Keyes]]'s ''[[Edge of Victory Duology]]'', and the eight novel in the ''[[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order]]'' series. ==Appearances== *''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory II: Rebirth]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The New Jedi Order Sourcebook]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Opeli Mors |es=Opeli Mors }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mors, Opeli}} [[Category:Criminals]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with she/her pronouns]]
